title: Many Mooblooms
description: A unique take on the Moobloom, adding 16 new variants!
body:
(Note: This mod was originally known as _Trash's Mooblooms_.)

This mod adds **Mooblooms**, the flower cow from Minecraft Earth!

In this mod, Mooblooms make their comeback with 16 variants, one for each color found in Minecraft. Furthermore, each Moobloom grows a completely new flower on its back! The list is as follows:

- White (Thunderbloom)
- Light Gray (Bellflower)
- Gray (Silver Iris)
- Black (Withercup)
- Brown (Boat Orchid)
- Red (Trillium)
- Orange (Butterfly Weed)
- Yellow (Buttercup)
- Lime (Conbush)
- Green (Gladioli)
- Cyan (Centian)
- Light Blue (Myosotis)
- Blue (Dayflower)
- Purple (Morning Glory)
- Magenta (Butterfly Candy)
- Pink (Tropical Hibiscus)

However, not every Moobloom is found naturally. You can only find Yellow, Blue, and Red Mooblooms naturally. Most other Mooblooms can only be obtained through breeding Mooblooms to mix their colors.

Like Mooshrooms, Mooblooms can be sheared to harvest the flowers on their back. Unlike Mooshrooms, Mooblooms do not convert to Cows once sheared. Rather, they regrow their flowers 20 minutes after they're sheared.

When you right click a Moobloom with a dyeable item, the item will be dyed!

Also, bees pollinate with the help of Mooblooms. Mooblooms appreciate this and hold still while they do their thing. :)


<details>
<summary>MODPACK / DATAPACK DEVS</summary>

This mod comes with a total of **17 biome tags.**
`#trashs_mooblooms:moobloom_spawnable`, along with one tag for each color with the following formatting: `#trashs_mooblooms:<color>_moobloom_spawnable`.

Biomes in `#trashs_mooblooms:moobloom_spawnable` will attempt to spawn mooblooms.

Biomes in `#trashs_mooblooms:<color>_moobloom_spawnable` will attempt to make a moobloom that spawns in the assigned biomes a specific color. This will be randomized if there are multiple. Also, biomes in these tags are automatically added to `#trashs_mooblooms:moobloom_spawnable`. If you add a biome to `#trashs_mooblooms:moobloom_spawnable` without it being in any of the other tags, the moobloom's color will default to yellow.

There is also a block tag, `#trashs_mooblooms:moobloom_spawnable_on`. These are the blocks that mooblooms can naturally spawn on. By default, this is set to `#minecraft:animals_spawn_on`.

</details>
